hero.name: Lee Ho Sing Cyrus
hero.title: Full Stack Developer
hero.tagline: Full-stack developer who excels where frontend and backend must work as one — integration, business logic, and delivery under pressure.

paragraphs: Full-stack application developer specialising in complex business systems, integrations, production operations and AI-assisted delivery.

Since 2026, I have led team AI adoption at FirmStudio — evaluating agent solutions, gathering colleague feedback, and rolling out company-wide AI-assisted workflows, with clear requirements and human communication at the centre.
highlights: Full-stack integration — developed KNSM, a multi-campus platform serving 20+ schools
Backend & crisis delivery — HKAB ops turnaround (20+ min to 5–10 min); DrugInSport 10k+ import
Led team AI adoption — evaluated tools, gathered feedback, and rolled out AI-assisted workflows company-wide

sections.title: Where the value sits
sections.text: Full-stack developer with 6 years of experience in Laravel, Vue, and JavaScript production systems. The clearest value lies in FE/BE integration, backend problem-solving, and delivery under pressure — not in competing with specialists on depth alone. Selected work includes KNSM, a Po Leung Kuk kindergarten platform serving 20+ schools; DrugInSport's 10,000+ drug-record import pipeline; and HKAB's 16-server on-premise operations revamp, where key procedures were cut from 20+ minutes to 5–10 minutes.

sections.title: Turning ambiguity into working systems
sections.text: I do my best work when requirements are not yet fully clear and the system needs someone who can connect UI flow, backend logic, and real user operations. KNSM was the clearest example: a Vue 3 and Laravel platform serving 20+ schools, where I helped turn unclear early requirements into workable flows with designers and PMs, then kept delivery moving through frequent demos and steady communication.

sections.title: Reliable when delivery gets difficult
sections.text: Over time, I have become the developer trusted with backend-heavy or difficult delivery work: large imports, production operations, deployment scripts, and troubled project takeovers. DrugInSport, HKAB, and similar work built my backend depth because the problems were not only technical — they required fast execution, clear reporting, and honest communication when trust was at risk.

sections.title: Designing for people who operate the system
sections.text: A recurring part of my work is turning complex business rules into systems that non-technical users can operate with confidence. Whether it is a CMS editor, school administrator, operations team, or POS user, I care about clear flows, visible feedback, sensible defaults, and documentation that supports real work instead of becoming a workaround for poor design.

sections.title: Led team AI adoption
sections.text: Since 2026, I have led FirmStudio's team AI adoption — evaluating agent solutions, gathering colleague feedback, and recommending Cursor for stable regional support in Hong Kong. The rollout built on practical wins such as AI-ready documentation from the Wellcome Lucky Draw revamp, extending AI-assisted workflows across daily development and team collaboration.

aiEra.kicker: On work, tools, and what remains human
aiEra.title: In the AI Era
aiEra.statement: AI changes how fast we can build, but the work itself has not changed. The real work is still understanding people, asking the right questions, shaping clear requirements, and designing systems that help non-technical users work better.

principles.label: 01 / Listen
principles.title: Start with the human need
principles.text: Before choosing tools or writing code, the important work is understanding what clients are actually trying to solve.

principles.label: 02 / Shape
principles.title: Turn ambiguity into workflow
principles.text: Clear requirements, UI flow, and business logic give AI the direction it needs to produce useful work.

principles.label: 03 / Build
principles.title: Use AI with judgment
principles.text: AI removes friction, but responsibility stays human: verify outputs, design sound systems, and make the final call.

experience.summary: Full-stack developer building multi-tenant internal systems, client web applications, CMS platforms, and production operation tooling.
experience.otherWork: Alongside the highlighted projects below, ongoing work includes CMS development & integration (HKAB is one example), internal tooling, client web maintenance, production deployment scripts, and AI workflow adoption across the team.

experience.summary: Full-stack web development across loyalty, e-commerce, booking, and POS-integrated online shop modules.
experience.otherWork: Additional loyalty, CMS, and client-specific web modules across the ECHK product suite.

projects.title: Wellcome Lucky Draw
projects.subtitle: Legacy Revamp, OCR Integration & AI-Ready Development Workflow
projects.summary: Legacy campaign codebase transformed into a safer AI-assisted delivery workflow through refactoring, Google OCR integration, and reusable technical documentation.
projects.description: A legacy campaign project maintained across 2025–2026. I took over its PHP and JavaScript codebase, removed duplicated and unsafe patterns, integrated Google OCR, and documented the real frontend and backend flows so AI-assisted changes could be made reliably and future delivery safer.
highlights: Reviewed and revamped a legacy PHP, JavaScript, jQuery, CSS, and MySQL codebase with duplicated logic and security weaknesses.
Integrated Google OCR to support new business requirements around receipt or document handling.
Created AI-friendly technical documentation that changed the project from AI-error-prone legacy code into a codebase AI agents could safely work on across frontend and backend.
Turned the documentation method into Cursor Skills, influencing the team's development workflow across other projects.

caseStudy.challengeApproach: The inherited code had repeated logic, security weaknesses, and implicit behaviour that made AI-generated changes risky. I first mapped the real flows, then reorganized the code, fixed vulnerabilities, integrated OCR for the new requirement, and wrote implementation documentation that agents could follow safely.
caseStudy.role: I took over the project in 2025 and led its 2026 review and revamp: frontend and backend code changes, OCR integration, security fixes, and the technical documentation for AI-assisted development.
caseStudy.outcome: The project moved from a legacy codebase that often confused AI agents to one where changes could be completed with much higher reliability. The documentation method was later generalized into Cursor Skills for the wider team.

visual.caption: Wellcome Lucky Draw: from legacy code to AI-assisted delivery

steps.title: Takeover
steps.text: Inherited the campaign project and learned the existing legacy behavior.

steps.title: Review & revamp
steps.text: Reorganized duplicated code and fixed vulnerabilities.

steps.title: OCR requirement
steps.text: Integrated Google OCR into the existing PHP and JavaScript flow.

steps.title: AI-friendly docs
steps.text: Documented frontend, backend, and workflow details so agents could work reliably.

steps.title: Reusable skill
steps.text: Generalized the documentation method into Cursor Skills for other projects.

projects.title: DrugInSport
projects.subtitle: Anti-Doping Drug Database
projects.summary: Public ADOHK drug lookup with a 10,000+ record import workflow, built end to end across JavaScript and Laravel backend.
projects.description: A public anti-doping drug lookup for ADOHK. I built the JavaScript and Laravel workflow behind 10,000+ drug records, using chunked import, memory tuning, validation, approval, and scheduled launch controls to keep complex updates reliable; the public UI was supplied by the design team.
highlights: Built all JavaScript (jQuery) and Laravel 11 backend logic end to end.
Designed chunked Excel import for 10,000+ drug records with memory optimization.
Implemented approval flow and scheduled launch for production release.

projects.title: KNSM
projects.subtitle: Po Leung Kuk Kindergarten Management System
projects.summary: Multi-campus kindergarten platform for 20+ schools, where I owned cross-module money logic, Oracle exports, and more than half of 30+ operational reports.
projects.description: A multi-campus internal platform for Po Leung Kuk kindergartens and nurseries. I delivered the interconnected billing, subsidy, refund, and reporting work across 20+ schools, with Oracle accounting exports and 30+ reports supporting daily operations; the platform remains in maintenance for schools to use daily.
highlights: Owned UI implementation and all money-related features — billing, subsidies, refunds, and fee settings across interconnected modules.
Built more than half of 30+ styled operational reports and Oracle AR/RA export integration.
Co-designed workflows when requirements were unclear; realigned with the team every other day under an unreasonably short timeline.
Maintained client trust through biweekly demos; praised by client and school principals.

caseStudy.challengeApproach: Requirements were incomplete after a PM change, schools handled the same processes differently, and the schedule was tight for money logic spanning enrollment, student records, care services, and finance exports. We realigned understanding every other day, surfaced gaps early, and shaped workable flows together as real school practices emerged.
caseStudy.role: I owned the UI implementation, fee settings and records, subsidies, refunds, usage-flow adjustments, Oracle AR/RA exports, and more than half of the 30+ reports. Because billing touched nearly every module, I coordinated the logic end to end rather than treating it as an isolated backend task.
caseStudy.outcome: Delivered a production platform serving 20+ campuses under extreme pressure, while biweekly demos kept client trust intact. The client and school principals praised the outcome.

projects.title: HKAB
projects.subtitle: Member Area & On-Premise Operations
projects.summary: Member portal and 16-server operations recovery: restored delivery confidence, then reduced a critical procedure from 20+ minutes to about 5.
projects.description: A member portal with granular permissions, CMS integration, and 16-server on-premise operations. I helped recover a delayed delivery, then rebuilt fragile operating procedures with clearer tooling and guides—cutting the main procedure from 20+ minutes to around 5; the portal remains in maintenance.
highlights: Built member area permissions and core portal features.
Revamped 16-server operation scripts in 2025, cutting key procedures from 20+ minutes to 5–10 minutes.
Restored a severely delayed project close to legal escalation through tight PM reporting.

caseStudy.challengeApproach: HKAB had two trust-critical moments: a late-stage takeover before launch, then a hidden operations flaw found two years later. We made recovery visible through weekly delivery and tight PM reporting; for operations, I traced the root cause and replaced slow PHP scripts with operator-focused shell menus, status, logs, warnings, and guides.
caseStudy.role: I built member-area permissions and core portal features, worked with the PM to restore delivery momentum, and later owned the maintenance and operations recovery work across the on-premise environment.
caseStudy.outcome: The 2025 ops revamp cut the main procedure from 20+ minutes to around 5 minutes, with guides operators could follow. Earlier weekly delivery also rebuilt client confidence through launch.

visual.caption: HKAB: two recoveries, one trust problem
visual.intro: HKAB was not a single clean delivery story. It had two separate moments where trust was at risk: first before launch, then again two years later during operations.

phases.label: Phase 01
phases.title: Delivery recovery
phases.prologue: The first recovery was about credibility. We could not change the fact that the project was late, so the only way forward was to make progress visible every week.
phases.description: The project was already late when I joined. Before solving features, we had to prove to the client that the team could still deliver.

steps.label: Late-stage takeover
steps.text: Joined when launch timing was already unrealistic.

steps.label: Brute-force recovery
steps.text: Daily extra hours and weekend focus to regain momentum.

steps.label: Weekly proof
steps.text: Showed visible progress, collected feedback, and adjusted fast.

steps.label: Trust restored
steps.text: The project stayed smooth through launch after confidence returned.

phases.label: Phase 02
phases.title: Ops recovery
phases.prologue: The second recovery was quieter but more dangerous. A hidden operations issue had stayed buried for two years, and the fix had to rebuild confidence with the people running the system.
phases.description: Two years later, a hidden operation flaw surfaced. The work shifted from feature delivery to root-cause fixing and operator-safe tooling.

steps.label: Hidden flaw surfaced
steps.text: A long-standing ops issue damaged trust with the hosting-side team.

steps.label: Root cause hunt
steps.text: Traced and fixed a problem nobody had noticed for two years.

steps.label: Script redesign
steps.text: Rebuilt slow PHP scripts into shell menus with status, colors, and logs.

steps.label: Operator-ready guide
steps.text: Cut the main procedure from 20+ minutes to about 5, with warning and error handling.

projects.title: 1O1O Corporate
projects.subtitle: CMS Revamp
projects.summary: CMS revamp that made publishing clearer through a component editor, approval workflow, version comparison, and form builder.
projects.description: A CMS revamp for 1O1O Corporate Solutions, delivered with another backend developer. I led the CMS UI/UX and built its component editor, version comparison, approval workflow, and form builder—making publishing choices clearer for content editors and setting a stronger standard for day-to-day publishing.
highlights: Designed and built CMS UI/UX for editors managing the corporate marketing site.
Component-based page editor for flexible page composition from reusable blocks.
Version compare and approval workflow for safer content publishing.
Form builder and detail-level polish across the CMS admin.

caseStudy.challengeApproach: The client expected a CMS that content editors could use confidently without relying on a manual. I shaped the admin experience around clear component choices, visible editing context, and safer publishing controls, while collaborating with another backend developer on the wider CMS revamp.
caseStudy.role: I owned the CMS UI and editor experience, and delivered roughly half of the backend scope: the component-based page editor, form builder, version comparison, approval workflow, and the in-context details that make content easier to understand before publishing.
caseStudy.outcome: The revised CMS gave editors a clearer, more guided publishing workflow. Its UI patterns and CMS guidance received strong feedback and became a reference point for future company CMS and design-system standards.

projects.title: DentalMiles
projects.subtitle: Loyalty & Booking Platform
projects.summary: Dental loyalty and booking platform with points, rewards, and eShop in one system.
projects.description: A single dental loyalty and booking platform combining points redemption, DentalMiles-to-CDE conversion, quota-based online booking, Miles Rewards, and eShop features. I implemented the loyalty redemption and booking flows that connected these customer journeys in one web system, bringing rewards and booking into one consistent customer experience.
highlights: Built points redemption and Miles Rewards flows.
Implemented online booking with quota rules and eShop features.

projects.title: HIPPOS
projects.subtitle: iPad POS, Online POS & E-Commerce Platform
projects.summary: Configurable multi-tenant POS and online shop platform with membership, points redemption, complex discounts, payments, member cards, and quota-based booking.
projects.description: A configurable multi-tenant platform linking iPad POS and online-shop operations. I owned POS APIs, online POS UI/UX, shop backend, database design, and member cards; membership, points, payments, discounts, and quota-based booking could be configured for new clients without hardcoded rules.
highlights: Built POS APIs and Online POS UI/UX features connecting iPad POS operations with the online shop.
Designed online shop backend and database structures for multi-tenant client configuration.
Implemented membership, points redemption, online payment, and member card functionality.
Supported user-configurable discount rules and quota-based schedule booking without hardcoded client logic.

education.institution: The Hong Kong Institute of Vocational Education (Tsing Yi)
